data.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent c81c plan-review fan-out epic; this child S1 reviewed whole against the full sibling roster]

4-bucket audit of parent acceptance/success criteria, mapping S1's contribution and cross-checking absences against the roster:

- "materially lower token cost" / "cache reads non-zero" — FULLY covered here (system-prompt + tool caching, AC: `usage.cache_read_input_tokens > 0`; described as the biggest single token win).
- "caching provider-safe: openai/gemini succeed unchanged" — FULLY covered here (anthropic-gated via `_anthropic_cache_settings`; per-provider verification AC).
- "make lint + suite pass" — per-child portion covered here.
- "completes in minutes (wall-clock)" — PARTIAL here (caching is the cost lever); wall-clock delegated to sibling ba7e (S3 parallelize). Covered by roster.
- "caching across plan-review AND all workflow agent steps" — PARTIAL here (runner/plan-review seam only; engine RunnerAgentStep explicitly Out-of-scope -> sibling c6e5/S2). Covered by roster.
- "no fidelity regression / within offline-eval tolerance" — STRUCTURAL here (fidelity-neutral by construction); semantic eval gating -> sibling da34/S6. Covered by roster.
- "all six child stories closed" — aggregate/structural; not a single-child mapping.

No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the union of children. G3: CLEAN — no findings for this pairing.
